Description: Raw building permit applications submitted by developers and contractors to municipal governments across Canada.
Notes: Primary origination point. Permit issuance triggers a 'housing start' classification when construction commences. Illustrative source system modelled on publicly documented housing-starts methodology.

Description: Field agents conduct monthly direct surveys of builders and developers to supplement permit data and capture starts not yet reflected in municipal records.
Notes: Illustrative source system. Records collected this way are flagged with SURVEY_METHOD = 'Direct Survey'.

Description: Monthly price survey aggregating individual transaction records for housing units started, inflation-adjusted using CPI reference year 2018. Source system for AVERAGE_PRICE_CAD.
Notes: Illustrative source system for AVERAGE_PRICE_CAD. The seeded DQ-004 sign-flip errors are attributed to this pipeline in the illustrative root cause analysis.

Description: Central ingestion layer that consolidates permit data from municipalities, survey responses from field agents, and administrative data from provincial registries. Applies initial validation and deduplication.
Notes: Illustrative ingestion layer. Deduplication key: GEO_CODE + REF_DATE + DWELLING_TYPE + INTENDED_MARKET. Records with STATUS='F' suppressed at this layer.

System_Name: Enterprise Data Quality Platform (e.g. Informatica IDMC)
Description: Applies enterprise data quality rules across completeness, validity, accuracy, and uniqueness dimensions. Flags records with DQ issues for steward review. Generates DQ scorecard metrics.
Notes: Illustrative processing layer representing a commercial DQ engine. Failed records written to a DQ exception table; rule results populate the DQ scorecard.

Description: All data assets, CDEs, data lineage, stewardship assignments, and governance policies are documented and managed in the enterprise catalog. Business glossary terms linked to physical columns.
Notes: Illustrative governance layer representing a commercial catalog platform. Stewardship workflows triggered for DQ issues.

Description: Open-source implementation of the DQ rule engine built for this project. Executes 15 SQL-equivalent rules across 5 dimensions, applies auto-remediation, and generates scorecard outputs and the Streamlit dashboard.
Output_Asset: DQ Scorecards + Exception Log + Remediated Dataset
Notes: The real implemented system in this project. Outputs: dq_exceptions.csv, cmhc_housing_starts_remediated.csv, scorecard CSVs.

System_Name: Public Open Data Repository (e.g. Statistics Canada CODR)
Description: Approved, quality-validated data published to a public open data platform. Data undergoes final suppression review before release.
Notes: Illustrative publication layer. The real public dataset this project is modelled after is Statistics Canada Table 34-10-0143-01. STATUS field reflects suppression flags.

Description: Executive dashboards tracking housing supply KPIs, provincial trends, and affordability indicators. Refreshed monthly from published dataset.
Notes: Illustrative consumer for policy reporting. Filters on STATUS != 'F' by default.

Description: Feeds housing supply data into federal housing affordability and policy reports.
Notes: Illustrative consumer. CDE-level lineage required for audit trail; corrections to source data must be traceable to published reports.

Description: Internal and external research teams consuming the published dataset via API or CSV extracts for forecasting, affordability modelling, and market research.
Notes: Illustrative consumer. Expected to filter on STATUS per data dictionary guidance.

Description: General public access to the published dataset through an open government data portal under the Open Government Licence – Canada.
Notes: Illustrative distribution channel referenced in the data contract terms (Open Government Licence – Canada).
